{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Löwenthal Variation| |rd| |bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|pd|pd| |pd| |pd|pd|pd| | |nd| | | | | | | | | |pd| | | | | | |nl|pl| | | | | | | | | | | |pl|pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl| |rl|| }} = Löwenthal Variation = The logic behind the move e5 is that Black, instead of delaying e5, hopes to resolve the issue of pawn structure early on. White has to move the d4 Knight; the test to Black's plan is the Nb5 line, putting pressure on the d6 square. ==Theory table== '''1.e4 c5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.d4 cxd4 4.Nxd4 e5'''
5 6 7
[[/5. Nb5|Nb5]]
d6		 N1c3
Nf6		 Bg5
a6		 +=
[[/5. Nxc6|Nxc6]]
bxc6		 Nc3
Nf6		 Bc4
Bb4		 =
[[/5. Nb3|Nb3]]
Nf6		 Bg5
h6		 =
